metadata.title: Miro Mariapori - Full-stack Software Developer
metadata.description: Junior full-stack software developer with strong React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, MongoDB, and backend experience. I build practical web products, APIs, integrations, automation, and data-driven tools.

structuredData.description: Junior full-stack software developer focused on React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, MongoDB, APIs, integrations, automation, and practical product development.

hero.name: Miro Mariapori
intro: I am a junior full-stack software developer focused on building practical web products with React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and modern backend systems.

My strongest experience comes from GameRAT, a 15-month healthcare software project where I independently designed and built the web platform, GraphQL API, authentication, MongoDB infrastructure, 3D exercise editor, movement-data pipeline, analysis tools, and supporting systems for Quest 3 physiotherapy data.

I enjoy turning complex requirements into working software, especially when the problem involves backend logic, data flow, architecture, or interactive tools. I am open to junior, internship, freelance, and full-time opportunities in Finland and remote roles across Europe.

whatIDo.title: What I do
description: I build user interfaces, backends, APIs, integrations, and automations that solve real needs. My main focus is full-stack product development: React and Next.js interfaces, Node.js services, GraphQL/REST APIs, MongoDB/PostgreSQL-backed applications, and reliable data flows.

I am strongest when a feature needs to work end to end. I can move from unclear requirements to a working implementation, connect the frontend and backend pieces, and keep the system maintainable after the first version.

Although React is one of my strongest skills, I am not looking for frontend-only work. I am most interested in full-stack or backend-leaning roles where I can contribute to architecture, APIs, data handling, and product functionality.

howIWork.title: How I work
description: I work in a structured and practical way. I value clear code, readable architecture, useful documentation, and solutions that work well from the user's perspective.

I am comfortable starting from zero, making technical decisions, and owning large parts of a system independently. In GameRAT, I was responsible for the web architecture, backend architecture, data model, authentication, and several complex product features.

I use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Codex, and Claude mainly for debugging and documentation, while keeping the technical understanding in my own hands. I read and verify generated code instead of treating it as a black box.
I am used to moving tasks forward independently, but I do my best work in teams where developers can discuss ideas openly, review each other's work, and improve solutions together.

experience.title: Experience
description: At Helsinki XR Center, I worked for 15 months on GameRAT, a healthcare software platform for gamifying physiotherapy exercises. I independently built the React web platform, GraphQL backend, JWT authentication, role-based access control, MongoDB infrastructure, 3D exercise editor, movement-data processing, messaging, pain-scale reporting, and analysis tools.

My thesis project was a TypeScript-based route management system for an autonomous tracked articulated vehicle. I built a React and Leaflet map interface, Node.js WebSocket communication layer, vehicle-specific command routing, and a simulator for testing multiple vehicles in real time.

As a freelance developer, I have delivered automations, websites, WordPress solutions, and custom software for individuals and student organizations. These projects have strengthened my ability to understand a customer problem, scope the implementation, and ship working solutions independently.

My strongest proof of work is GameRAT: a 15-month healthcare platform where I built the web app, backend, authentication, 3D exercise editor, and Quest 3 movement-data pipeline. I am looking for a junior full-stack or backend-leaning role where I can build reliable web products for real users.

ProjectsPage.title: Projects
ProjectsPage.description: Selected projects that show practical implementation, technical ownership, and my ability to build complex features and working products from unclear requirements.

projects.name: Gamified Joint Rehabilitation, Analysis and Training (GameRAT)
projects.summary: A 15-month healthcare software project where I independently built the web platform, GraphQL backend, MongoDB infrastructure, 3D exercise editor, movement-data pipeline, analysis tools, messaging, and supporting systems for Quest 3 physiotherapy data.
description: GameRAT was built to gamify physiotherapy exercises and give physiotherapists tools to assign, monitor, and analyze patient exercises.

I independently designed and built the full web platform and backend from scratch, including the React frontend, GraphQL API, JWT authentication, role-based access control, MongoDB data model, and server-side infrastructure.

Built the physiotherapist interface for login, patient creation and editing, assigning pre-made or saved exercises, viewing 3D exercise previews, reviewing completion status, messaging with patients, and collecting pain-scale feedback for each exercise.
Created a custom Three.js-based 3D exercise editor where physiotherapists could manipulate arm joints with sliders and save reusable keyframe-based exercise animations in an intuitive workflow.
Designed a custom JSON movement-data format and parser that converted raw Quest 3 hand-tracking data into browser-playable Three.js animation data for movement analysis.
Implemented Unity-facing API interfaces for patient authentication, exercise data exchange, and recorded movement-data submission from Quest 3 applications.
Built a separate .NET data-collection application and backend for recording good and bad physiotherapy movement samples used to train a custom neural-network system from scratch.
Implemented sensitive-data handling measures, including role-based access boundaries, backend-only database access, and RSA encryption for patient-related data.
projects.role: Independently responsible for the web platform, backend, database infrastructure, authentication, 3D exercise editor, movement-data pipeline, and neural-network support systems.

projects.name: Route Management System for an Autonomous Tracked Articulated Vehicle
projects.summary: A TypeScript thesis system for visual route creation, vehicle-specific command routing, and real-time monitoring of autonomous tracked vehicle simulations.
description: Built the system as a thesis project to enable visual creation and management of routes for an autonomous tracked articulated vehicle.
The system was designed as a step from direct remote-control operation toward route-based autonomous control, while full collision-avoidance autonomy remained outside the thesis scope.
Built a React and Leaflet map interface where users can add, move, and remove route markers while the route is automatically drawn between GPS points.
Represented routes with GeoJSON and route metadata such as route name and point types including start, mid, and end points.
Implemented a TypeScript Node.js WebSocket server for real-time communication, vehicle-specific identifiers, command routing, and status updates.
Built a vehicle simulator that maintains its own GPS position and heading in memory, reacts to commands like the real vehicle would, and follows route commands as a simplified GPS line follower.
Added a safety behavior where lost connection events send a stop signal to all connected vehicles.
Validated the system with multiple simultaneous simulated vehicles and documented the implementation for future development.
projects.role: Sole developer responsible for the frontend, backend, WebSocket protocol, simulator, documentation, and system architecture.

projects.name: TKIO-Finder
projects.summary: A full-stack matching platform for helping Metropolia students and staff discover and connect around RDI project positions.
description: Built a full-stack platform to solve the problem that many Metropolia RDI project positions were open to students and staff, but difficult to discover.
Implemented a lightweight matching workflow where students can like a project position, attach a short message, and match with the project owner if both sides show interest.
Designed the database model for users, jobs, applications, and matches using PostgreSQL.
Implemented full-text search and filters such as location and position type to make project opportunities easier to browse.
Added Google OAuth2 authentication and restricted usage to Metropolia-managed email accounts, with separate roles for students, staff, and admins.
Implemented profile and job-posting CRUD features for students and staff users.
Deployed the project to a private server with Nginx, PM2, reverse proxy configuration, HTTPS, domain setup, server process management, and a CI/CD pipeline.
Received the highest grade for the project.
projects.role: Mainly responsible for backend development, database design, authentication, deployment, and infrastructure.

projects.name: Personal Portfolio
projects.summary: A multilingual Next.js portfolio designed to present my projects, technical ownership, CV, and contact details clearly for recruiters and technical reviewers.
description: Designed and developed a multilingual portfolio website for presenting my projects, skills, CV, and contact details
Built a responsive interface with CSS Modules, keeping the layout simple, maintainable, and easy to scan
Added page-specific metadata, canonical and alternate language links, and JSON-LD structured data for SEO
Deployed the site on Vercel and added Vercel Analytics for lightweight visitor insights
